texto: There are tales that rise like the early sun, breathe, and take on a life of their own. There are ones that flow quietly and effortlessly until time forsakes them, but there are others that fight until they find their way to the edge of reality, as if coming straight out of a dream.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Edwin Fontanez: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “There are tales that rise like the early sun, breathe, and take on a life of their own. There are…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, boy, cat pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de cat e fiction.

There are ones that flow quietly and effortlessly until time forsakes them, but there are others that fight until they find their way to the edge of reality, as if coming straight out of a dream.
